top_signals.title: MOLLE, PALS, and the Empty Loops of America’s Civil-Military Divide
top_signals.summary:
Editor’s note: This is the third article in a limited series celebrating American defense technologies born from wartime and their effects on broader national security, politics, and society. This series will run for several weeks to commemorate America’s 250th anniversary, and winners will be selected by a reader vote undertaken through our newsletter later this summer. Prior installments can be found at the Arsenal of Innovation page. In the half-century since the Gates Commission established the all-volunteer force — and especially over the last 25 years — the relationship between the American soldier and the state has changed profoundly. Military service has become

The post MOLLE, PALS, and the Empty Loops of America’s Civil-Military Divide appeared first on War on the Rocks.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
top_signals.summary: Intruder built an AI-powered "vulnerability vending machine" that combines code slicing with LLMs to automatically discover complex software vulnerabilities. The company explains how the system found and exploited a previously unknown WordPress plugin zero-day, with additional discoveries already under responsible disclosure. [...]

top_signals.title: BulkLoads expands ag freight footprint with Livestock Network acquisition
top_signals.summary:
BulkLoads has acquired Livestock Network, adding the livestock-focused load board and online community to its freight marketplace.

The post BulkLoads expands ag freight footprint with Livestock Network acquisition  appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.
